# 14 — Datagen Patterns

## Why datagen matters

Hand-written JSON for recipes, tags, lang, and content profiles drifts. A typo in `data/colony/job/farmer.json` breaks the mod silently until someone opens that file. Datagen generates these files from Java code, validated by the compiler.

**Rule:** anything that goes into `src/main/resources/data/` or `src/main/resources/assets/` and is project-controlled (not third-party datapack) is generated by datagen.

Exceptions:

- Tag files for items/blocks owned by other mods (we contribute, don't author the whole tag).
- Compat patches that depend on third-party mod existence.

## Datagen module

Datagen runs as a separate Gradle run config (`runData`), not as a sub-module. The generation classes live in `:neoforge/src/main/java/com/akikazu/colony/neoforge/datagen/`.

Outputs go to `:neoforge/src/generated/resources/`. The `processResources` task includes this directory automatically.

### Why not a `:datagen` module

Hearthbound considered datagen as a separate source set. We don't, because:

- Datagen classes need access to all the content registrations.
- A separate module adds Gradle plumbing for marginal benefit.
- The `runData` configuration already isolates execution from the main jar.

A source set named `datagen` could be used if classpath separation becomes important. Defer that decision.

## Generators

NeoForge 1.21.1 datagen uses `DataProvider` implementations. Each provider generates one category of files.

### Required providers (V1)

| Provider | Generates | Location |
|---|---|---|
| `ColonyBlockTagsProvider` | block tag JSONs | `data/colony/tags/blocks/` |
| `ColonyItemTagsProvider` | item tag JSONs | `data/colony/tags/items/` |
| `ColonyRecipeProvider` | crafting recipes for mod items | `data/colony/recipes/` |
| `ColonyLootTableProvider` | loot tables for blocks/entities | `data/colony/loot_tables/` |
| `ColonyLangProvider` | translation JSON | `assets/colony/lang/en_us.json` |
| `ColonyBuildingTypeProvider` | building type profile JSONs | `data/colony/building_type/` |
| `ColonyRoomTypeProvider` | room type profile JSONs | `data/colony/room_type/` |
| `ColonyJobTypeProvider` | job type profile JSONs | `data/colony/job_type/` |
| `ColonyAnchorTypeProvider` | anchor type profile JSONs | `data/colony/anchor_type/` |
| `ColonyFilterProvider` | filter profile JSONs | `data/colony/filter/` |
| `ColonyFunctionalBlockDetectorProvider` | detector profile JSONs | `data/colony/functional_block_detector/` |
| `ColonyTraitProvider` | trait profile JSONs | `data/colony/trait/` |

Registered in `ColonyDataGenerator` (extends NeoForge's `GatherDataEvent` handler).

### Pattern: tag provider

```java
public final class ColonyBlockTagsProvider extends BlockTagsProvider
{
    public ColonyBlockTagsProvider(PackOutput output, CompletableFuture<HolderLookup.Provider> lookup,
        ExistingFileHelper helper)
    {
        super(output, lookup, ColonyMod.MODID, helper);
    }

    @Override
    protected void addTags(HolderLookup.Provider lookup)
    {
        tag(ColonyTags.Blocks.BEDS)
            .addTag(BlockTags.BEDS);

        tag(ColonyTags.Blocks.WALL_ART)
            .add(Blocks.PAINTING)
            .addOptional(modRef("create", "framed_glass"));

        tag(ColonyTags.Blocks.HAZARD)
            .add(Blocks.LAVA)
            .add(Blocks.MAGMA_BLOCK)
            .add(Blocks.CAMPFIRE)
            .add(Blocks.SOUL_CAMPFIRE);
    }

    private ResourceLocation modRef(String namespace, String path)
    {
        return ResourceLocation.fromNamespaceAndPath(namespace, path);
    }
}
```

`ColonyTags.Blocks` is a static class holding `TagKey<Block>` constants. Centralized to avoid string drift.

### Pattern: profile provider

For content profiles (BuildingType, RoomType, etc.), we use a custom base class `ColonyProfileProvider<T>`:

```java
public abstract class ColonyProfileProvider<T> implements DataProvider
{
    protected final PackOutput.PathProvider pathProvider;
    protected final Codec<T> codec;

    protected ColonyProfileProvider(PackOutput output, String registryPath, Codec<T> codec)
    {
        this.pathProvider = output.createPathProvider(PackOutput.Target.DATA_PACK, registryPath);
        this.codec = codec;
    }

    @Override
    public final CompletableFuture<?> run(CachedOutput cache)
    {
        Map<ResourceLocation, T> entries = new LinkedHashMap<>();
        generate(entries::put);

        List<CompletableFuture<?>> futures = new ArrayList<>();

        for (Map.Entry<ResourceLocation, T> entry : entries.entrySet())
        {
            Path path = pathProvider.json(entry.getKey());
            JsonElement json = codec.encodeStart(JsonOps.INSTANCE, entry.getValue())
                .getOrThrow(false, msg -> { throw new IllegalStateException("Codec failed for " + entry.getKey() + ": " + msg); });

            futures.add(DataProvider.saveStable(cache, json, path));
        }

        return CompletableFuture.allOf(futures.toArray(CompletableFuture[]::new));
    }

    protected abstract void generate(BiConsumer<ResourceLocation, T> consumer);
}
```

Concrete provider:

```java
public final class ColonyJobTypeProvider extends ColonyProfileProvider<JobProfile>
{
    public ColonyJobTypeProvider(PackOutput output)
    {
        super(output, "colony/job_type", JobProfile.CODEC);
    }

    @Override
    protected void generate(BiConsumer<ResourceLocation, JobProfile> consumer)
    {
        consumer.accept(
            ResourceLocation.fromNamespaceAndPath("colony", "farmer"),
            new JobProfile(
                Identifier.of("colony", "harvesting"),
                Identifier.of("colony", "tree_chopping_alike"),
                1,
                List.of(Identifier.of("colony", "filter/farming_tools")),
                List.of(),
                List.of(Identifier.of("colony", "trait/green_thumb")),
                16,
                0.0f,
                List.of(Identifier.of("colony", "anchor/scarecrow")),
                Identifier.of("colony", "room_tier/basic")));

        consumer.accept(
            ResourceLocation.fromNamespaceAndPath("colony", "builder"),
            new JobProfile(/* ... */));
    }
}
```

The codec validates the structure at generation time. A typo in field names doesn't compile. A missing required field throws at gen time.

### Pattern: lang provider

```java
public final class ColonyLangProvider extends LanguageProvider
{
    public ColonyLangProvider(PackOutput output)
    {
        super(output, ColonyMod.MODID, "en_us");
    }

    @Override
    protected void addTranslations()
    {
        // Blocks
        add(ColonyBlocks.TOWN_HALL.get(), "Town Hall");
        add(ColonyBlocks.RESIDENCE_HUT.get(), "Residence Hut");
        add(ColonyBlocks.FARMER_HUT.get(), "Farmer Hut");

        // Items
        add(ColonyItems.COLONY_TOOL.get(), "Colony Tool");
        add(ColonyItems.SCARECROW.get(), "Scarecrow");

        // Entities
        add(ColonyEntities.CITIZEN.get(), "Citizen");

        // GUI strings
        addGui("tab.overview", "Overview");
        addGui("tab.structure", "Structure");
        addGui("tab.storage", "Storage");
        addGui("tab.workzones", "Work Zones");
        addGui("tab.citizens", "Citizens");
        addGui("button.assign_worker", "Assign worker");
        addGui("button.designate", "Designate");
        addGui("button.link", "Link");
        addGui("button.unlink", "Unlink");
        addGui("label.tier", "Tier: %s");
        addGui("label.structural_score", "Structural: %s");
        addGui("error.zone_too_small", "Zone is too small (%s blocks, minimum %s)");
        addGui("error.encloses_hut_block_failed", "Zone must contain the hut block");
        addGui("warning.over_capacity", "Over capacity: %s residents in tier %s building");

        // Messages
        addMessage("colony_founded", "Colony \"%s\" founded.");
        addMessage("zone_wand_required", "You need a Colony Tool to place hut blocks. Use /colony wand restore.");

        // Trait names
        addTrait("strong", "Strong");
        addTrait("strong.desc", "Bonus to physical labor jobs (Mining, Lumber).");
        addTrait("green_thumb", "Green Thumb");
        addTrait("green_thumb.desc", "Bonus to farming productivity.");

        // Room types
        addRoomType("bedroom", "Bedroom");
        addRoomType("kitchen", "Kitchen");
        addRoomType("dining_room", "Dining Room");
        addRoomType("office", "Office");
        addRoomType("storage_room", "Storage Room");
    }

    private void addGui(String key, String value)
    {
        add("colony.gui." + key, value);
    }

    private void addMessage(String key, String value)
    {
        add("colony.message." + key, value);
    }

    private void addTrait(String key, String value)
    {
        add("colony.trait." + key, value);
    }

    private void addRoomType(String key, String value)
    {
        add("colony.room_type." + key, value);
    }
}
```

Helper methods enforce the naming convention from `09-GLOSSARY.md`. No raw `add("colony.gui.tab.overview", ...)` calls. Always via a helper.

### Pattern: recipe provider

```java
public final class ColonyRecipeProvider extends RecipeProvider
{
    public ColonyRecipeProvider(PackOutput output, CompletableFuture<HolderLookup.Provider> lookup)
    {
        super(output, lookup);
    }

    @Override
    protected void buildRecipes(RecipeOutput output)
    {
        ShapedRecipeBuilder.shaped(RecipeCategory.MISC, ColonyBlocks.SCARECROW.get())
            .pattern(" S ")
            .pattern("SBS")
            .pattern(" S ")
            .define('S', Items.STICK)
            .define('B', Blocks.HAY_BLOCK)
            .unlockedBy("has_hay", has(Blocks.HAY_BLOCK))
            .save(output);

        ShapedRecipeBuilder.shaped(RecipeCategory.MISC, ColonyBlocks.TOWN_HALL.get())
            .pattern("LLL")
            .pattern("LGL")
            .pattern("OOO")
            .define('L', ItemTags.LOGS)
            .define('G', Items.GOLD_INGOT)
            .define('O', Blocks.OAK_PLANKS)
            .unlockedBy("has_gold", has(Items.GOLD_INGOT))
            .save(output);
    }
}
```

Standard NeoForge recipe builder. No surprises.

## Running datagen

```
./gradlew runData
```

Outputs are written to `src/generated/resources/`. Re-run after every content change.

CI verification:

- The build calls `./gradlew runData` first.
- Then checks `src/generated/resources/` against the committed version.
- If they differ, the build fails with a message: "Datagen output diverges from committed. Run `./gradlew runData` locally and commit the changes."

This forces contributors to commit generated files (so the artifact ships with them) but ensures the generated files match the Java sources.

## Compatibility with other mods

For tags that overlap with other mods, use `addOptional`:

```java
tag(ColonyTags.Items.SEEDS)
    .addTag(ItemTags.SEEDS)
    .addOptional(ResourceLocation.fromNamespaceAndPath("farmersdelight", "tomato_seeds"))
    .addOptional(ResourceLocation.fromNamespaceAndPath("create", "wheat_flour_seeds"));
```

`addOptional` doesn't fail if the mod isn't present. No hard dependency.

## Profile JSON migrations and datagen

When a profile schema changes (a `JobProfile` field added/removed):

1. The codec is updated in `:api`.
2. The Java provider is updated to match.
3. `./gradlew runData` regenerates the JSON files.
4. The new JSON files are committed.
5. A migration step is added to `:common/persistence/migration/steps/` for older saves.

The datagen ensures all shipped profiles match the current schema. Migration ensures existing saves load.

## What's NOT in datagen (V1)

- World generation features. None planned.
- Damage type registrations. None custom in V1.
- Advancement files. No achievement system in V1.
- Loot table modifications for vanilla blocks. We don't touch vanilla.
- Worldgen biome modifiers. Out of scope.

V2 may add advancements if a progression system materializes.

## Custom assets datagen (V2 candidate)

Currently we hand-write:

- Block models and blockstates.
- Item models.
- Texture references.

This is fine for V1's small content set (5-7 blocks, 3-4 items, 1 entity).

V2 may add `BlockStateProvider` and `ItemModelProvider` if content grows. Defer until pain is felt.
